question: What is the common operating frequency for Low Frequency (LF) RFID cards?
High Frequency (HF) RFID cards typically operate at 125 kHz.
Which Proxmark command do you run to test whether a card responds on the LF antenna?
After confirming a card responds at HF, which command attempts to identify its technology family?
What command starts the Proxmark client?
LF proximity cards are generally considered more complex and secure than modern HF smart cards.
Which of the following is a typical use case for HF RFID technology?
options: Legacy building entry systems from the 1990s
Modern enterprise badges and NFC-based systems
